steps: Recognize \(x^2 - 100y^2\) as a difference of squares: \(a^2 - b^2 = (a-b)(a+b)\) with \(a = x\) and \(b = 10y\).

Evaluate every choice:
  • A: \(11(x-2y)(x-50y)\) — expanding gives \(11(x^2 - 52xy + 100y^2)\), which has the wrong sign on \(100y^2\) and an extra \(xy\) term. Eliminated.
  • B: \(11(x-2y)(x+50y)\) — expanding gives \(11(x^2 + 48xy - 100y^2)\), wrong factors entirely. Eliminated.
  • C: \(11(x-10y)(x-10y)\) — this is \(11(x-10y)^2 = 11(x^2 - 20xy + 100y^2)\), a perfect square, not a difference. Eliminated.
  • D: \(11(x-10y)(x+10y)\) — matches the derived factorization exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
